title: context.rs: apply_prompt_modification silently replaces last user message — no audit trail, no consent
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Functional Correctness / Error Orchestration. Location: src/context.rs:54-64 (apply_prompt_modification). Violation: A hook returning a modified_prompt causes the user's actual message to be silently overwritten with the hook's version. There is no indication to the user that their prompt was rewritten, no log of the substitution, and no diff preserved. A malicious or buggy hook can fully hijack user intent — e.g., 'delete the database' where the user said 'list the tables'. Additionally, find+replace loop uses iter_mut().rev().find(...) — if no user message exists, the modification is silently dropped with no error. Mandated Refactor: (a) Log every prompt modification at INFO with before/after (truncated if large). (b) Preserve the original in a hidden system message appended after the modification: '<original-user-prompt>...</original-user-prompt>'. (c) Return Result<bool, ContextError> so the caller knows whether modification actually occurred.
